_comment_gripper: closed raised from 0.01 -> 0.015 (per-finger; gap = 2x) so the fingers stop on the ~3 cm clock_needle_crank knob instead of driving through it (was closing too far).

clock_needle_minute._comment: Non-colliding shape rigidly part of the crank's body in CoppeliaSim: glue it onto clock_needle_crank so it turns with the revolute joint instead of spawning standalone.

Revolute_joint._comment: clock (static base) <-> clock_needle_crank (dynamic). PhysX IGNORES joint 'friction' for non-articulation joints, so firmness comes from an angular DAMPING drive (target_velocity 0, no stiffness): it resists wobble/off-axis jitter and holds the hand where the gripper leaves it, yet a slow turn still overcomes it. Raise 'damping' if it still flops, lower it if the arm can't turn it.
